morning.text: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of [all] existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
morning.fadilah: Recite in the morning: Protection from Jinn until the evening. Recite in the evening: Protection from Jinn until the morning.
morning.title: Ayat al-Kursi

morning.text: Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent.'
morning.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
morning.title: Surah Al-Ikhlas

morning.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies.'
morning.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
morning.title: Surah Al-Falaq

morning.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the Jinn and mankind.'
morning.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
morning.title: Surah An-Nas

morning.text: We have reached the morning and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. My Lord, I ask You for the good of this day and the good of what follows it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil of this day and the evil of what follows it. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from laziness and the evil of old age. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from punishment in the Hell-fire and punishment in the grave.
O Allah, by Your leave we have reached the morning and by Your leave we have reached the evening, by Your leave we live and by Your leave we die, and unto You is our resurrection.

O Allah, You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped but You. You created me and I am Your slave, and I am faithful to my covenant and my promise as much as I can. I seek refuge in You from all the evil I have practiced. I acknowledge before You all the blessings You have bestowed upon me, and I confess to You all my sins. So forgive me, for indeed none can forgive sins except You.
morning.fadilah: Master of Seeking Forgiveness (Sayyid al-Istighfar). Whoever recites it in the morning with firm faith and dies that day will enter Paradise.

morning.text: O Allah, I have reached the morning and I call upon You and the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels and all Your creation to witness that You are Allah, none has the right to be worshipped but You alone, You have no partner, and that Muhammad is Your slave and Messenger.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it 4 times in the morning or evening, Allah will set him free from the Hell-fire.

morning.text: O Allah, whatever blessing has reached me or any of Your creation this morning, it is from You alone with no partner. To You is all praise and to You is all thanks.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it in the morning has fulfilled his thanks for that day.

morning.text: O Allah, grant me health in my body. O Allah, grant me health in my hearing. O Allah, grant me health in my sight. None has the right to be worshipped but You. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave. None has the right to be worshipped but You.
Allah is sufficient for me. None has the right to be worshipped but He. In Him I put my trust and He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it 7 times, Allah will suffice him for whatever concerns him.

morning.text: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the next. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion and my worldly affairs, my family and my wealth. O Allah, veil my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, protect me from before me and from behind me, from my right and from my left, and from above me, and I seek refuge in Your greatness from being swallowed up from below me.

O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the evident, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord of everything and its Possessor, I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own self and from the evil of Shaytan and his shirk, and from committing any wrong against my own self or bringing it upon another Muslim.
In the Name of Allah, Who with His Name nothing is harmed on earth nor in heaven, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times, nothing will harm him.

morning.text: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as my Prophet.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times in the morning and evening, Allah will surely please him on the Day of Resurrection.

morning.text: O Ever-Living, O Self-Subsisting, by Your mercy I seek assistance. Correct for me all of my affairs and do not leave me to myself even for the blink of an eye.

We have reached the morning and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, the Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of this day: its victory, its support, its light, its blessings and its guidance. And I seek refuge in You from the evil of what is in it and the evil of what follows it.

We have reached the morning upon the natural religion of Islam, the word of pure faith, the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) and the way of our father Ibrahim, who was a person of pure belief and a Muslim, and he was not of those who associate partners with Allah.
Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him, by the number of His creation and His pleasure, and by the weight of His Throne and the ink of His words.
O Allah, I ask You for knowledge that is of benefit, a good provision and deeds that are accepted.
I seek refuge in Allah's perfect words from the evil of what He has created.
morning.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times in the evening will be protected from harmful stings that night.

morning.text: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and grief, and I seek refuge in You from inability and laziness, and I seek refuge in You from cowardice and miserliness, and I seek refuge in You from the burden of debt and from being overpowered by men.

O Allah, You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped but You. In You I put my trust and You are the Lord of the Mighty Throne. What Allah wills happens and what He does not will does not happen. There is no might nor power except with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great. I know that Allah is Able to do all things and that Allah has encompassed all things with His knowledge. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own self and from the evil of every creature You take by the forelock. Indeed, my Lord is on a straight path.
morning.fadilah: Protection from calamities morning to evening and vice versa.

morning.text: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.
morning.fadilah: Whoever sends blessings upon me 10 times in the morning and 10 times in the evening will attain my intercession on the Day of Resurrection.

morning.text: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him.
morning.fadilah: His sins will be forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea.

morning.text: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things.
morning.fadilah: Reward equivalent to freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds written, 100 sins erased, and protection from Shaytan until the evening.

morning.text: I seek Allah's forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance.

evening.text: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of [all] existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it in the evening will be protected from Jinn until the morning.
evening.title: Ayat al-Kursi

evening.text: Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent.'
evening.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
evening.title: Surah Al-Ikhlas

evening.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies.'
evening.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
evening.title: Surah Al-Falaq

evening.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the Jinn and mankind.'
evening.fadilah: Recite 3 times: Sufficient for you against everything.
evening.title: Surah An-Nas

evening.text: We have reached the evening and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. My Lord, I ask You for the good of this night and the good of what follows it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil of this night and the evil of what follows it. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from laziness and the evil of old age. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from punishment in the Hell-fire and punishment in the grave.
O Allah, by Your leave we have reached the evening and by Your leave we have reached the morning, by Your leave we live and by Your leave we die, and unto You is our return.

O Allah, You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped but You. You created me and I am Your slave, and I am faithful to my covenant and my promise as much as I can. I seek refuge in You from all the evil I have practiced. I acknowledge before You all the blessings You have bestowed upon me, and I confess to You all my sins. So forgive me, for indeed none can forgive sins except You.
evening.fadilah: Master of Seeking Forgiveness. Whoever recites it during the evening with firm faith and dies that night will enter Paradise.

evening.text: O Allah, I have reached the evening and I call upon You and the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels and all Your creation to witness that You are Allah, none has the right to be worshipped but You alone, You have no partner, and that Muhammad is Your slave and Messenger.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it 4 times in the evening, Allah will set him free from the Hell-fire.

evening.text: O Allah, whatever blessing has reached me or any of Your creation this evening, it is from You alone with no partner. To You is all praise and to You is all thanks.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it in the evening has fulfilled his thanks for that night.

evening.text: O Allah, grant me health in my body. O Allah, grant me health in my hearing. O Allah, grant me health in my sight. None has the right to be worshipped but You. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave. None has the right to be worshipped but You.
Allah is sufficient for me. None has the right to be worshipped but He. In Him I put my trust and He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it 7 times, Allah will suffice him for whatever concerns him.

evening.text: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the next. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion and my worldly affairs, my family and my wealth. O Allah, veil my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, protect me from before me and from behind me, from my right and from my left, and from above me, and I seek refuge in Your greatness from being swallowed up from below me.

O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the evident, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord of everything and its Possessor, I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own self and from the evil of Shaytan and his shirk, and from committing any wrong against my own self or bringing it upon another Muslim.
In the Name of Allah, Who with His Name nothing is harmed on earth nor in heaven, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times, nothing will harm him.

evening.text: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as my Prophet.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times, Allah will surely please him on the Day of Resurrection.

evening.text: O Ever-Living, O Self-Subsisting, by Your mercy I seek assistance. Correct for me all of my affairs and do not leave me to myself even for the blink of an eye.

We have reached the evening and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, the Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of this night: its victory, its support, its light, its blessings and its guidance. And I seek refuge in You from the evil of what is in it and the evil of what follows it.

We have reached the evening upon the natural religion of Islam, the word of pure faith, the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) and the way of our father Ibrahim, who was a person of pure belief and a Muslim, and he was not of those who associate partners with Allah.
I seek refuge in Allah's perfect words from the evil of what He has created.
evening.fadilah: Whoever recites it 3 times in the evening will be protected from harmful stings that night.

evening.text: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.
evening.fadilah: Whoever sends blessings upon me 10 times in the morning and 10 times in the evening will attain my intercession on the Day of Resurrection.

evening.text: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him.
evening.fadilah: His sins will be forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea.

evening.text: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things.
evening.fadilah: Reward equivalent to freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds written, 100 sins erased, and protection from Shaytan until the morning.

evening.text: I seek Allah's forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance.

sleep.text: In Your name, my Lord, I lay my side down, and by You I raise it. If You take my soul, have mercy on it, and if You release it, protect it as You protect Your righteous servants.
O Allah, You created my soul and You take it. Its death and its life are for You.

mosque.text: In the name of Allah, and praise be to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, forgive me my sins and open the gates of Your mercy for me.
In the name of Allah, and praise be to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, forgive me my sins and open the gates of Your bounty for me.

post_prayer.text: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of [all] existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
post_prayer.fadilah: Whoever recites it after every prescribed prayer, nothing will prevent him from entering Paradise except death.
post_prayer.title: Ayat al-Kursi

post_prayer.text: Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent.'
post_prayer.title: Surah Al-Ikhlas

post_prayer.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies.'
post_prayer.title: Surah Al-Falaq

post_prayer.text: Say, 'I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the Jinn and mankind.'
post_prayer.title: Surah An-Nas

post_prayer.text: I seek Allah's forgiveness (3 times). O Allah, You are Peace and from You comes Peace. Blessed are You, O Owner of Majesty and Honor.

None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. O Allah, there is no preventer of what You grant and there is no granter of what You prevent, and no fortune of a fortunate person will benefit him against You.

None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. There is no might nor power except with Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, and we do not worship besides Him. His is grace, and His is bounty and His is noble praise. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, we are sincere in faith to Him, though the disbelievers may detest it.
None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, He gives life and causes death and He is Able to do all things.
post_prayer.fadilah: To be said after Fajr and Maghrib prayers.

post_prayer.text: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things.
